root_text.an1.53:1.3: ‘bhikkhu arittajjhāno viharati satthusāsanakaro ovādapatikaro, amoghaṁ raṭṭhapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati’.
root_text.an1.53:1.4: Ko pana vādo ye naṁ bahulīkarontī”ti.
root_text.an1.54:1.3: ‘bhikkhu arittajjhāno viharati satthusāsanakaro ovādapatikaro, amoghaṁ raṭṭhapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati’.
root_text.an1.54:1.4: Ko pana vādo ye naṁ bahulīkarontī”ti.
root_text.an1.55:1.1: “Accharāsaṅghātamattampi ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu mettācittaṁ manasi karoti;
root_text.an1.55:1.3: ‘bhikkhu arittajjhāno viharati satthusāsanakaro ovādapatikaro amoghaṁ raṭṭhapiṇḍaṁ bhuñjati’.
root_text.an1.55:1.4: Ko pana vādo ye naṁ bahulīkarontī”ti.
root_text.an1.56:1.1: “Ye keci, bhikkhave, dhammā akusalā akusalabhāgiyā akusalapakkhikā, sabbe te manopubbaṅgamā.
root_text.an1.56:1.2: Mano tesaṁ dhammānaṁ paṭhamaṁ uppajjati, anvadeva akusalā dhammā”ti.
root_text.an1.57:1.1: “Ye keci, bhikkhave, dhammā kusalā kusalabhāgiyā kusalapakkhikā, sabbe te manopubbaṅgamā.
root_text.an1.57:1.2: Mano tesaṁ dhammānaṁ paṭhamaṁ uppajjati, anvadeva kusalā dhammā”ti.
root_text.an1.58:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā akusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā vā kusalā dhammā parihāyanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, pamādo.
root_text.an1.58:1.2: Pamattassa, bhikkhave, anuppannā ceva akusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā ca kusalā dhammā parihāyantī”ti.
root_text.an1.59:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā kusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā vā akusalā dhammā parihāyanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, appamādo.
root_text.an1.59:1.2: Appamattassa, bhikkhave, anuppannā ceva kusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā ca akusalā dhammā parihāyantī”ti.
root_text.an1.60:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā akusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā vā kusalā dhammā parihāyanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, kosajjaṁ.
root_text.an1.60:1.2: Kusītassa, bhikkhave, anuppannā ceva akusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā ca kusalā dhammā parihāyantī”ti.

translation_text.an1.51:1.2: But it is corrupted by passing corruptions.
translation_text.an1.51:1.3: An unlearned ordinary person does not truly understand this.
translation_text.an1.51:1.4: So I say that the unlearned ordinary person has no development of the mind.”
translation_text.an1.52:1.2: And it is freed from passing corruptions.
translation_text.an1.52:1.3: A learned noble disciple truly understands this.
translation_text.an1.52:1.4: So I say that the learned noble disciple has development of the mind.”
translation_text.an1.53:1.1: “If, mendicants, a mendicant cultivates a mind of love even as long as a finger-snap,
translation_text.an1.53:1.3: a mendicant who does not lack absorption, who follows the Teacher’s instructions, who responds to advice, and who does not eat the country’s alms in vain.
translation_text.an1.53:1.4: How much more so those who make much of it!”
translation_text.an1.54:1.1: “If, mendicants, a mendicant develops a mind of love even as long as a finger-snap,
translation_text.an1.54:1.3: a mendicant who does not lack absorption, who follows the Teacher’s instructions, who responds to advice, and who does not eat the country’s alms in vain.
translation_text.an1.54:1.4: How much more so those who make much of it!”
translation_text.an1.55:1.1: “If, mendicants, a mendicant focuses on a mind of love even as long as a finger-snap,
translation_text.an1.55:1.3: a mendicant who does not lack absorption, who follows the Teacher’s instructions, who responds to advice, and who does not eat the country’s alms in vain.
translation_text.an1.55:1.4: How much more so those who make much of it!”
translation_text.an1.56:1.1: “Mendicants, intention shapes all phenomena whatsoever that are unskillful, part of the unskillful, on the side of the unskillful.
translation_text.an1.56:1.2: Intention is the first of those phenomena to arise, and unskillful phenomena follow right behind.”
translation_text.an1.57:1.1: “Mendicants, intention shapes all phenomena whatsoever that are skillful, part of the skillful, on the side of the skillful.
translation_text.an1.57:1.2: Intention is the first of those phenomena to arise, and skillful phenomena follow right behind.”
translation_text.an1.58:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to unskillful qualities, or makes skillful qualities decline like negligence.
translation_text.an1.58:1.2: When you’re negligent, unskillful qualities arise and skillful qualities decline.”
translation_text.an1.59:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to skillful qualities, or makes unskillful qualities decline like diligence.
translation_text.an1.59:1.2: When you’re diligent, skillful qualities arise and unskillful qualities decline.”
translation_text.an1.60:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to unskillful qualities, or makes skillful qualities decline like laziness.
translation_text.an1.60:1.2: When you’re lazy, unskillful qualities arise and skillful qualities decline.”

variant_text.an1.53:1.1: mettācittaṁ → mettaṁ cittaṁ (bj); mettacittaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr)
